req.title: System-Admin views performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:admin
performance-review module is enabled

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-024 requires: (1) authenticated system-admin can view a performance-review via GET /api/performance-reviews/{id}, receiving HTTP 200/201 with entity id; (2) mandatory field validation returns 400; (3) unauthenticated access returns 401; (4) forbidden role returns 403; (5) audit record is written on success. Test design covers: EP for the happy-path admin role, NEG for unauthenticated and forbidden-role paths, BVA for a boundary identifier (id=1, the minimum valid integer PK), DT for the authentication x authorisation decision table, and STC for the state transition from 'review created' to 'review viewed + audit written'.

tests.name: test_admin_can_view_performance_review_returns_200
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated and holds role:admin
Performance-review module is enabled
A performance-review record exists with a known id
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <admin_auth_fixture>
Capture response status code and body
expected: Response body contains the review entity id matching the requested id
An audit record for this view action exists in the audit log
trace: R-WEB-024: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-024: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_can_view_performance_review_returns_200(
    admin_auth_headers,
    existing_review_id,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    url = f"{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}"
    response = requests.get(url, headers=admin_auth_headers)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(existing_review_id), (
        "Response entity id does not match requested review id"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=existing_review_id,
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "No audit record written for successful view"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: No Authorization header is present in the request
Performance-review module is enabled
A performance-review record exists with a known id
actions: Send GET /api/performance-reviews/{review_id} with NO Authorization header
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    existing_review_id,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    url = f"{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}"
    response = requests.get(url)

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=existing_review_id,
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, (
        "Audit record must NOT be written for unauthenticated access"
    )

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: A non-admin authenticated user (role:employee) holds a valid token
Performance-review module is enabled
A performance-review record exists with a known id
actions: Obtain a valid token for a user with role:employee via <employee_auth_fixture>
Send GET /api/performance-reviews/{review_id} with employee Authorization header
expected: No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-024: only system-admin actor is permitted
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_returns_403(
    employee_auth_headers,
    existing_review_id,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    url = f"{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/{existing_review_id}"
    response = requests.get(url, headers=employee_auth_headers)

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=existing_review_id,
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, (
        "Audit record must NOT be written for forbidden role access"
    )

tests.name: test_view_performance_review_with_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated and holds role:admin
Performance-review module is enabled
A performance-review record with id=1 (minimum valid integer PK) exists
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <admin_auth_fixture>
Capture response status code and body
expected: Response body contains id equal to 1
An audit record for this view action is written
trace: R-WEB-024: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-024: audit record written on success
BVA: minimum boundary of the id domain (id=1)
tests.executable:
import requests

MIN_REVIEW_ID = 1

def test_view_performance_review_with_minimum_boundary_id(
    admin_auth_headers,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
    ensure_review_exists,
):
    ensure_review_exists(MIN_REVIEW_ID)
    url = f"{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/{MIN_REVIEW_ID}"
    response = requests.get(url, headers=admin_auth_headers)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201 for id=1, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(MIN_REVIEW_ID), (
        f"Expected entity id=1, got {body.get('id')}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=MIN_REVIEW_ID,
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record missing for boundary id=1 view"

tests.name: test_view_nonexistent_review_returns_400_or_404
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated and holds role:admin
Performance-review module is enabled
No performance-review record exists with id=999999999
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <admin_auth_fixture>
expected: Response status is 400 or 404 (validation / not-found error)
Response body contains an error descriptor
trace: R-WEB-024: audit record only written on success
tests.executable:
import requests

NONEXISTENT_ID = 999999999

def test_view_nonexistent_review_returns_400_or_404(
    admin_auth_headers,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    url = f"{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/{NONEXISTENT_ID}"
    response = requests.get(url, headers=admin_auth_headers)

    assert response.status_code in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400/404, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body or "detail" in body, (
        "Response body must contain an error descriptor"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=NONEXISTENT_ID,
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, (
        "Audit record must NOT be written when review is not found"
    )

tests.name: test_audit_record_state_transition_after_successful_view
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated and holds role:admin
Performance-review module is enabled
A performance-review record exists (state: created/active)
No prior view audit entry exists for this review in this test run
actions: Confirm no pre-existing view audit entry for the review via <audit_log_client>
Send GET /api/performance-reviews/{review_id} with admin Authorization header
Verify audit log transitions from 0 entries to >= 1 entry for action=view
expected: Audit log entry count for this review+action transitions from 0 to at least 1
Audit entry contains actor, timestamp, resource id, and action fields
trace: R-WEB-024: audit record written on success
STC: state transition from review-created (no audit) to review-viewed (audit present)
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_audit_record_state_transition_after_successful_view(
    admin_auth_headers,
    isolated_review_id,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    pre_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=isolated_review_id,
    )
    assert len(pre_entries) == 0, "Precondition: no prior audit entries for this review"

    url = f"{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/{isolated_review_id}"
    response = requests.get(url, headers=admin_auth_headers)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)

    post_entries = audit_log_client.fetch_latest(
        action="view",
        resource="performance-review",
        resource_id=isolated_review_id,
    )
    assert len(post_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must exist after successful view"

    entry = post_entries[0]
    for field in ("actor", "timestamp", "resource_id", "action"):
        assert field in entry, f"Audit entry missing required field: {field}"
